target_flow: AI agent records itself performing a task, then edits the footage into a polished demo video using its own action log as the edit decision list
architecture_note: The agent has perfect knowledge of its own action timeline — every UAB call is timestamped. This log serves as the timecode/EDL for editing. No vision is needed to understand what is in the video because the agent authored every frame.

phase_1_prepare.description: Set up screen recording before performing the demo task

steps.action: Start a screen recorder. Options: OBS (hotkey Ctrl+F9 to start), Xbox Game Bar (Win+Alt+R), or PowerShell Start-Process with ffmpeg.
Note the recording start timestamp as T0. All action timestamps will be relative to T0.
Optionally set the target app window to a clean size/position for a good frame.

phase_2_perform.description: Execute the demo task. Every UAB action is logged with a timestamp.

steps.action: Perform the task (control Outlook, build Excel dashboard, chat with Grok, etc.)
For each action, record: {timestamp, action, target, result, duration_ms}
Deliberately pause 1-2 seconds between major steps — gives clean cut points
If an error occurs, note the timestamp — this is a cut point for editing

phase_3_stop_recording.description: Stop the screen recorder and note the final timestamp

steps.action: Stop the screen recorder (OBS: Ctrl+F9, Game Bar: Win+Alt+R)
Note the recording file path and total duration
Calculate duration: T_end - T0

phase_4_generate_edl.description: Use the action log to create an edit decision list — which segments to keep, cut, or trim

steps.action: Review the action log for errors, long pauses, or retries — mark as CUT
Identify the clean action sequences — mark as KEEP
Add 0.5s padding before and after each KEEP segment for smooth transitions
Note any moments worth highlighting (first successful action, impressive speed, etc.)

example.label: Compose and send email — hero sequence

phase_5_edit_in_capcut.description: Open the recording in CapCut and apply the EDL using keyboard shortcuts
phase_5_edit_in_capcut.prerequisite: Load the capcut flow library for full shortcut reference

steps.action: hotkey
Drag clip to timeline or double-click to add
keypress
For each CUT segment in the EDL:
sub_steps: Navigate to the cut start time using Right arrow (frame by frame) or L key (shuttle forward)
Ctrl+B to split at the start of the cut
Navigate to the cut end time
Ctrl+B to split at the end of the cut
Click the cut segment, press Delete to remove it

steps.action: keypress
steps.note: Zoom to fit timeline — verify the edit

steps.action: keypress
keypress
hotkey

phase_5_edit_in_capcut.time_navigation_tip: At 30fps, each Right arrow press = 1 frame = 33ms. To navigate to a specific second, calculate: frames = seconds * 30. Use Shift+Right for larger jumps. Use J/K/L shuttle for fast scrubbing.

frame_math.example: To reach 3.5 seconds at 30fps: press Right arrow 105 times, or use L to shuttle forward and K to stop near the target

phase_6_output.description: The final exported video is a polished demo created entirely by the AI agent
deliverables: Action log (JSON) — complete record of what was demonstrated
EDL (JSON) — the edit decisions made and why

apps_involved.screen_recorder: OBS, Xbox Game Bar, or ffmpeg
apps_involved.demo_target: Any app UAB can control (Outlook, Excel, Grok, etc.)
apps_involved.uab: Provides action timestamps as the edit decision source

why_this_works: The agent does not need to 'watch' the video to edit it. It authored every action in the recording and has timestamped logs of exactly what happened when. The action log IS the timecode. The agent is simultaneously the performer, the cinematographer, and the editor — with perfect memory of the performance.
known_issues: Frame-accurate navigation requires knowing the recording FPS
CapCut elements are unnamed — all editing is keyboard-shortcut based
Screen recorder must be started/stopped cleanly — agent should verify recording file exists
Long recordings may need J/K/L shuttle instead of frame-by-frame arrow navigation
If the demo involves multiple monitors, the recording region must cover the right screen
